 {"id":"bd-10vp","title":"Feature: Extension Event Hook Dispatch","description":"# Feature: Extension Event Hook Dispatch

## Overview

This feature enables the agent to call INTO extensions at lifecycle events. Extensions register handlers via pi.on(eventName, handler), and the agent dispatches events at appropriate points.

## Distinction from Events Hostcall

- **Events Hostcall (pi.events)**: Extension-initiated, asks runtime about events
- **Event Hook Dispatch**: Agent-initiated, calls extension handlers at lifecycle points

## Event Types (from EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md §12.7)

### Agent Lifecycle Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| startup | Agent initialized | Access session, configure |
| agent_start | Before first API call | Modify system prompt |
| agent_end | After agent loop ends | Log, cleanup |

### Turn Lifecycle Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| turn_start | Before provider.stream() | Modify context |
| turn_end | After response processed | Log, analyze |

### Tool Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| tool_call | Before tool execution | Block, modify |
| tool_result | After tool execution | Modify result |

### Session Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| session_before_switch | Before session switch | Cancel switch |
| session_before_fork | Before session fork | Cancel fork |

### Input Events

| Event | When | Handler Can |
|-------|------|-------------|
| input | Before processing user message | Transform, block |

## Event Handler Return Values

Different events allow different return values:

```typescript
// tool_call: Can block execution
interface ToolCallEventResult {
    block?: boolean;
    reason?: string;
}

// tool_result: Can modify the result
interface ToolResultEventResult {
    content?: ContentBlock[];
    details?: unknown;
}

// session events: Return false to cancel
type SessionEventResult = boolean;
```

## Implementation Architecture

```
Agent Loop (src/agent.rs)
    │
    ├─── dispatch_event("turn_start", context)
    │           │
    │           ▼
    │    PiJsRuntime.__pi_dispatch_extension_event()
    │           │
    │           ▼
    │    [Extension handlers called in sequence]
    │           │
    │           ▼
    │    Results aggregated and returned
    │
    └─── [Continue with turn]
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All documented events dispatch at correct points
- [ ] Blocking events (tool_call) can prevent execution
- [ ] Modifying events (tool_result) can change data
- [ ] Cancellable events (session_*) return boolean
- [ ] Handler errors don't crash agent
- [ ] Extension context passed correctly to handlers","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:58.456544979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:09:31.483635020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:09:31.483570760Z","close_reason":"All 7 sub-tasks complete: EventDispatcher created (bd-tg4w), agent lifecycle events (bd-13gm), turn lifecycle events (bd-5yyc), tool_call dispatch (bd-35hz), tool_result dispatch (bd-318h), input event (bd-s1hx), session events (bd-3avm). All event types dispatch at correct lifecycle points with fail-open error handling.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10vp","depends_on_id":"bd-30zg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-118dw","title":"[SEC-3.2A] Runtime-risk quantile hot-path optimization (selection + scratch reuse) with proof artifacts","description":"## Background
Runtime risk decisions currently run per-hostcall, so even moderate per-decision allocation/sort overhead compounds into latency and CPU drag.

## Scope
- Optimize quantile path from full-sort to selection-based order statistic.
- Reuse per-extension scratch buffers to remove repeat allocations.
- Preserve deterministic behavior and existing risk decisions.
- Capture isomorphism proof and before/after benchmark artifacts.

## Deliverables
- Hot-path optimization in runtime risk evaluator.
- Evidence pack: baseline benchmark, post-change benchmark, invariance notes.
- Linked references to changed code paths and tests.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Measurable latency reduction on runtime-risk decision path.
- [ ] No behavior drift in targeted runtime-risk tests.
- [ ] Proof artifacts are reproducible and attached.","notes":"Canonical grounding: alien_cs_graveyard.md §12.1 (conformal quantile hot-path optimization), §0.4 (expected-loss decision core), §0.18 (anytime-valid e-process), §0.19 (evidence ledger). EV=(4*4*5)/(2*1)=40. Primary risk: constants/edge-order drift; countermeasure: deterministic test replay + baseline comparator + fallback-safe behavior preserved.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T05:22:20.288189516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:25:37.885737063Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T05:23:44.959989912Z","close_reason":"Implemented in src/extensions.rs runtime risk path; switched quantile to select_nth_unstable_by and reused residual scratch buffer, then validated via targeted tests + before/after hyperfine.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","runtime-detection","security","statistics"]}
 {"id":"bd-11cz","title":"PiWasm: Spec WebAssembly JS subset + glue patterns","description":"# Goal
Lock the *minimum sufficient* WebAssembly JS API surface for PiWasm (WebAssembly-in-JS) to support the pinned extension corpus without Node/Bun.

# Why
- QuickJS does not ship WebAssembly; the bridge must be explicit, deterministic, capability-gated, and testable.
- A crisp spec prevents accidental Node/Bun scope creep.

# Deliverables
- Supported API list (MVP): WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports), WebAssembly.compile(bytes) (optional), WebAssembly.Module/Instance (if needed), WebAssembly.Memory (required).
- Explicitly unsupported APIs (with required error shapes + diagnostics).
- Glue compatibility matrix: common JS+WASM bundle patterns (Emscripten-style) we intend to support and their required semantics.
- Determinism + security invariants: budgets, import gating, no ambient OS.

# Acceptance
- Spec is detailed enough to implement without consulting external notes.
- Includes example call shapes + expected failure mapping.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:24.163710747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:50:17.799626703Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:50:17.799535242Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11cz","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2630,"issue_id":"bd-11cz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## PiWasm JS API Spec (Final)

### Supported APIs (MVP)

#### WebAssembly.compile(bufferSource) → Promise<Module>
- Accepts: Uint8Array, ArrayBuffer, or array of bytes
- Returns: A compiled Module handle
- Errors: WebAssembly.CompileError on invalid bytes

#### WebAssembly.instantiate(bufferSource, importObject?) → Promise<{module, instance}>
- Accepts: WASM bytes + optional import object
- Import object: { module_name: { func_name: func, ... }, ... }
- Returns: { module: Module, instance: Instance }
- Overload: instantiate(Module, importObject?) → Promise<Instance>
- Errors: WebAssembly.LinkError on import mismatch

#### WebAssembly.Instance
- instance.exports → object with exported funcs + memory

#### WebAssembly.Memory
- new Memory({initial, maximum?}) → standalone memory
- memory.buffer → ArrayBuffer (snapshot of linear memory)
- memory.grow(delta) → previous page count (or -1 on failure)

#### WebAssembly.RuntimeError / CompileError / LinkError
- Standard error constructors for WASM trap/compile/link failures

### Explicitly Unsupported (MVP)
- instantiateStreaming/compileStreaming → NotSupportedError
- Table, Global, validate → not yet supported error
- Shared memory / atomics → not supported

### Memory Sync Protocol
1. After instantiate: copy wasmtime memory → JS ArrayBuffer
2. Before export call: copy JS ArrayBuffer → wasmtime memory
3. After export call: copy wasmtime memory → JS ArrayBuffer
4. On grow: create new larger ArrayBuffer, old reference stale

### Import Handling (MVP - core polyfill only)
- Module imports satisfied by generic Rust stubs (return 0/no-op)
- Full JS callback imports deferred to bd-ltk7
- WASI/Emscripten-specific stubs in bd-ltk7

### Security Invariants
- Memory limited to max_memory_pages from policy
- Structured tracing logs for compile/instantiate
- No raw OS access from WASM execution
- All operations capability-gated","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:50:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-11k","title":"Implement provider streaming tests using VCR cassettes","description":"# Implement provider streaming tests using VCR cassettes

## Goal
Write comprehensive provider streaming tests using recorded VCR cassettes (no live network).

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests.
- Prefer `#[asupersync::test]` or an explicit `Runtime::new().block_on(...)` wrapper to match the active runtime.
- Force `VCR_MODE=playback` + `VCR_CASSETTE_DIR` for all tests.
- Set `PI_CONFIG_PATH` and `PI_SESSIONS_DIR` to temp dirs; assert zero live HTTP calls.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log cassette name/path, request summary, event timeline, and diffs.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual event sequences + stop reasons.

## Test File Structure
```rust
// tests/provider_streaming.rs

mod vcr;
use vcr::VcrRecorder;

mod anthropic {
    use super::*;

    #[asupersync::test]
    async fn test_simple_text_response() {
        let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("anthropic_simple_text");
        let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

        let stream = provider.stream(&context, &options).await.unwrap();
        let events: Vec<_> = stream.collect().await;

        assert!(matches!(events[0], StreamEvent::Start { .. }));
        assert!(matches!(events[1], StreamEvent::TextStart { .. }));
        // ... verify all events
        let final_msg = events.last().unwrap();
        assert_eq!(final_msg.stop_reason, StopReason::Stop);
    }
}
```

## Test Categories

### Streaming Event Sequence Tests
Verify correct event ordering:
- Start → TextStart → TextDelta* → TextEnd → Done
- Start → ThinkingStart → ThinkingDelta* → ThinkingEnd → TextStart → ... → Done
- Start → ToolCallStart → ToolCallDelta* → ToolCallEnd → Done(ToolUse)

### Content Parsing Tests
Verify content is parsed correctly:
- Text content extraction
- Thinking content extraction
- Tool call argument parsing (JSON)
- Multiple content blocks

### Usage Tracking Tests
Verify token counting:
- Input tokens
- Output tokens
- Cache read/write tokens
- Cost calculation

### Error Handling Tests
Verify errors are surfaced correctly:
- Rate limit with retry-after
- Auth failure message
- Bad request details
- Server error handling

## Providers to Test
1. Anthropic (primary focus, most complex)
2. OpenAI (after Anthropic pattern established)
3. Gemini (different response format)
4. Azure (similar to OpenAI)

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure)
- bd-30u (Anthropic cassettes recorded)

## Files
- tests/provider_streaming.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/anthropic.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/openai.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/gemini.rs
- tests/provider_streaming/azure.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20+ Anthropic streaming tests
- [ ] 10+ OpenAI streaming tests
- [ ] 10+ Gemini streaming tests
- [ ] 5+ Azure streaming tests
- [ ] All tests use VCR (no live API)
- [ ] Event sequences validated
- [ ] Usage tracking validated
- [ ] Error handling validated
- [ ] Logs include cassette path + event timeline
- [ ] All tests pass in CI","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayBear","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:00.703077638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:08:03.068865719Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T19:08:03.068830022Z","close_reason":"Validated and completed: existing provider streaming VCR suite meets acceptance counts and passes targeted tests/gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11k","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-11k","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-11k","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-11k","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3528,"issue_id":"bd-11k","author":"codex","text":"Added provider streaming integration tests with VCR playback/record scaffolds: 20 Anthropic scenarios, 10 OpenAI, 10 Gemini, 6 Azure. Shared helpers in tests/provider_streaming.rs for stream summary + expectations + logging. Tests skip when cassette missing unless VCR_STRICT=1. Waiting on bd-30u cassette recording (API keys currently unset).","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:34:37Z"},{"id":3529,"issue_id":"bd-11k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation pass (GrayBear, 2026-02-06):\n- Existing suite already satisfies target test volume: anthropic=20, openai=10, gemini=10, azure=6 (counted from provider_streaming modules).\n- VCR playback run passed end-to-end: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_graybear VCR_MODE=playback VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=tests/fixtures/vcr cargo test --test provider_streaming -- --nocapture -> 89 passed, 0 failed.\n- Focused compile/lint gates passed for this bead surface: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_graybear cargo check --test provider_streaming; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_graybear cargo clippy --test provider_streaming -- -D warnings.\n- Repository-wide cargo fmt --check currently fails due pre-existing unrelated formatting diffs in multiple files outside bd-11k scope.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:07:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11mqo","title":"[SEC-5.3] Incident evidence bundle export and forensic replay UX","description":"## Background
Incident response needs deterministic, minimally sufficient artifacts that preserve privacy.

## Scope
- Export incident bundles containing relevant ledger slices, policy snapshots, and redacted traces.
- Provide replay utility to reconstruct timeline and decisions.
- Define redaction and retention controls.

## Deliverables
- Bundle format spec and export CLI/RPC path.
- Replay command docs and validation checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bundle generation is deterministic for same incident scope.
- [ ] Sensitive data is redacted per policy.
- [ ] Replay can reproduce key enforcement events for triage.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:42.577950149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T11:53:19.378563455Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T11:53:19.378463258Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["forensics","incident-response","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11mqo","depends_on_id":"bd-3i9da","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:15Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-11mqo","depends_on_id":"bd-qudx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:15Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3910,"issue_id":"bd-11mqo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OpusAgent claiming bd-11mqo (SEC-5.3). Will implement: (1) incident evidence bundle format with deterministic generation, (2) ledger slice + policy snapshot + redacted trace export, (3) replay utility for decision timeline reconstruction, (4) redaction/retention controls integrated with secret broker policy.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:06:50Z"},{"id":3911,"issue_id":"bd-11mqo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-5.3 implementation complete. Added:

**Types** (src/extensions.rs):
- `IncidentBundleFilter`: time-window, extension-id, alert category, min severity filtering
- `IncidentBundleRedactionPolicy`: 6-field privacy control (params_hash, context_hash, args_shape_hash, command_hash, name_hash, remediation)
- `IncidentEvidenceBundle`: self-contained bundle with all 6 artifact types + replay + summary + integrity hash
- `IncidentBundleSummary`: 10-field quick triage (counts, peak risk, deny count, chain integrity)
- `IncidentBundleVerificationReport`: 6-field integrity report

**Functions** (src/extensions.rs):
- `build_incident_evidence_bundle()`: deterministic bundle construction with filtering + redaction
- `compute_incident_bundle_hash()`: SHA-256 integrity seal over all content sections
- `verify_incident_evidence_bundle()`: schema + hash + chain + summary cross-checks
- `export_incident_bundle()` on ExtensionManager: convenience method collecting all artifacts

**Tests** (tests/incident_evidence_bundle.rs): 30 integration tests covering:
- Construction, deterministic generation, time/extension/category/severity filtering
- Default + custom redaction, integrity verification, replay, JSON roundtrip, chain integrity

All 30 tests pass. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:49:36Z"},{"id":3912,"issue_id":"bd-11mqo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified SEC-5.3 complete: 142 tests pass (30 unit + 112 integration).  {"id":"bd-11pg","title":"Tests: message queue semantics (steering/follow-up)","description":"# Goal
Add deterministic automated tests for message queue semantics.

# Scope
- Queue ordering
- Delivery boundaries
- one-at-a-time vs all modes
- abort + restore behavior
- dequeue behavior

# Testing Strategy
- Prefer state-machine tests in `tests/tui_state.rs` (or similar) that feed messages/keys and assert resulting state.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage is sufficient to prevent regressions in queue delivery ordering.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
